evals.name: zero-touch-post-all-no-linear
evals.prompt:
Auto-post these panel-review findings to PR 90 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: c0ffee123). Linear is NOT reachable from this session. Just post the legitimate ones, no triage. Dry run: do NOT call gh api — write per-comment payloads to ./comments.json, planned reactions to ./reactions.json, and the disposition report to ./report.md.

Codex (HIGH, security)
- apps/api/src/auth/cookie.ts:30 — Session cookie set without `secure`; the single line `  res.cookie('sid', token, { httpOnly: true })` (2-space indent) should be `  res.cookie('sid', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true })`. Self-contained one-line replacement.
  Fix: replace that line with `  res.cookie('sid', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true })`.

Claude (MEDIUM, architecture)
- apps/api/src/services/report.ts:120 — The service formats currency inline instead of using the shared money formatter; should route through the existing `formatMoney` util.
  Fix: use formatMoney(amount) rather than the inline toFixed call.

Claude (LOW, polish)
- apps/api/src/utils/log.ts:8 — Log line omits the request id; the single line `  logger.info(msg)` (2-space indent) should be `  logger.info({ reqId }, msg)`.
  Fix: replace with `  logger.info({ reqId }, msg)`.

All three lines are inside the PR diff. Both code fixes are confident and clearly about this PR's changes.
evals.expected_output: This is ZERO-TOUCH: the skill must NOT call AskUserQuestion at all (no select lists, no confirmation) — it applies the bar automatically and writes the dry-run files. comments.json is a flat array of exactly 3 per-comment payloads (no review wrapper, no `event`/`comments[]` nesting), each with `commit_id: "c0ffee123"`, `path`, and line targeting. Severity is NOT a gate, so the LOW finding IS posted. Bodies carry NO severity, NO `Recommendation:` line, NO priority, NO panelist attribution. (1) The codex HIGH (cookie.ts:30) is a clean single-line drop-in, so its body MUST carry a ```suggestion block with exactly `  res.cookie('sid', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true })` (2-space indent), single-line comment anchored to `line: 30`, `side: "RIGHT"`. (2) The claude MEDIUM (report.ts:120) is conceptual/architectural — NOT a faithful drop-in — so its body uses a `**Possible Solution:**` prose line and NO suggestion block. (3) The claude LOW (log.ts:8) is a clean single-line drop-in, so it carries a ```suggestion block with exactly `  logger.info({ reqId }, msg)`, AND because it is LOW/polish its body is prefixed `Small / Optional polish:` (or equivalent). No body has BOTH a suggestion and a prose fix line. Order in comments.json is HIGH→MEDIUM→LOW: cookie.ts (HIGH) first, report.ts (MEDIUM) second, log.ts (LOW) last. reactions.json is an empty array (no existing comments were provided to dedupe against). No linear_tickets.json content is required (Linear unreachable) — if written it must be an empty array. report.md records the PR resolution line (PR #90, resolved from context), all three findings as posted (noting suggestion vs prose for each), and that there were no Linear-bound findings.

evals.name: dedupe-thumbs-up-existing-bot-comment
evals.prompt:
Auto-post these findings to PR 145 in catena-labs/api-service (head SHA: ab99ff1). Linear not reachable. Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./reactions.json, ./report.md — do not call gh api to post, but assume you fetched existing PR comments and found this one already there:

EXISTING inline review comment (from coderabbitai[bot], user.type=Bot) on apps/api/src/charges/refund.ts:42, html_url https://github.com/catena-labs/api-service/pull/145#discussion_r555 , body: "Refund amount isn't validated as positive — a negative value would create a charge."

Findings to act on:

Codex (HIGH, correctness)
- apps/api/src/charges/refund.ts:42 — Refund amount allows negative values, which would create a charge instead of a refund. Validate amount > 0 before processing.
  Fix: guard with `if (amount <= 0) throw new BadRequest('amount must be positive')`.

Codex (MEDIUM, correctness)
- apps/api/src/charges/capture.ts:70 — Capture uses `==` for the status check; should be `===`. The line `  if (charge.status == 'authorized')` should be `  if (charge.status === 'authorized')`.
  Fix: replace with `  if (charge.status === 'authorized')`.

Both lines are in the diff.
evals.expected_output: Zero-touch, no AskUserQuestion. The skill must run the single dedupe pass and recognize the codex HIGH (refund.ts:42) as the SAME issue as the existing coderabbitai[bot] comment (same file, same line, same underlying problem — negative refund amount). So it MUST NOT post a duplicate for that finding: comments.json contains exactly 1 payload — only the capture.ts:70 MEDIUM. reactions.json contains exactly 1 entry for the refund.ts finding: a planned +1 (`content=+1`) on the existing inline comment, referencing comment surface=inline, the existing html_url (…/pull/145#discussion_r555) and author coderabbitai (a Bot). The capture.ts:70 payload is a clean single-line drop-in so its body carries a ```suggestion block with exactly `  if (charge.status === 'authorized')` (2-space indent), anchored to `line: 70`. Neither output dedupes against any human or self comment. report.md records refund.ts as '+1'd existing automated comment (coderabbitai)' and capture.ts as posted (suggestion).

evals.name: auto-route-uncertain-and-out-of-scope-to-linear
evals.prompt:
Auto-post panel-review findings for PR 210 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: 7e7e7e7). Linear IS reachable; file any Linear-bound findings under team "Platform" project "Code Health". Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./linear_tickets.json, ./report.md, no real API calls.

Codex (HIGH, correctness)
- apps/web/src/checkout/total.ts:55 — Off-by-one in tax rounding on the changed line; `Math.floor(tax)` should be `Math.round(tax)`. The line `  const taxCents = Math.floor(tax)` should be `  const taxCents = Math.round(tax)`.
  Fix: replace with `  const taxCents = Math.round(tax)`.

Claude (MEDIUM, reliability)
- apps/web/src/checkout/retry.ts:88 — This MIGHT double-charge under a specific concurrent-retry race, but it depends on how the upstream gateway dedupes idempotency keys, which isn't visible in this diff and needs investigation before a fix can be designed.
  Fix: unclear — needs to confirm gateway idempotency behavior first.

Opencode (MEDIUM, tech-debt)
- apps/web/src/legacy/cart.ts:12 — Pre-existing N+1 query in the legacy cart loader. This file is NOT touched by this PR; the review just noticed it.
  Fix: batch the per-item lookups.

total.ts:55 is in the diff. retry.ts:88 is in the diff but the concern is unverifiable from the diff. cart.ts is not part of this PR.
evals.expected_output: Zero-touch, no AskUserQuestion. Routing: (1) the codex HIGH (total.ts:55) is confident and in-scope → PR. comments.json has exactly 1 payload (total.ts:55), commit_id `7e7e7e7`, carrying a ```suggestion block with exactly `  const taxCents = Math.round(tax)` anchored to line 55. (2) The claude MEDIUM (retry.ts:88) is UNCERTAIN / needs investigation (explicitly can't be confirmed from the diff) → Linear, NOT the PR. (3) The opencode MEDIUM (cart.ts:12) is real but OUT OF PR SCOPE (untouched legacy file) → Linear, NOT the PR. So linear_tickets.json has exactly 2 tickets, both with team "Platform", project "Code Health", NO priority field, and descriptions containing the PR link + file:line deep-link, File:, Severity:, then the body, then the fix as a `**Possible Solution:**` prose line (NOT a ```suggestion block, even for cart.ts whose fix is code — suggestions are GitHub-inline-only). Neither uncertain nor out-of-scope finding appears in comments.json. report.md records total.ts as posted (suggestion), retry.ts as filed-to-Linear (reason: uncertain/needs investigation), cart.ts as filed-to-Linear (reason: out of PR scope), and the PR resolution line for PR #210.

evals.name: override-low-polish-to-linear
evals.prompt:
Auto-post these findings to PR 33 in catena-labs/api-service (head SHA: d3d3d3). Linear IS reachable — put any Linear items in team "API" project "Triage". The user override: post the LOW/polish issues to Linear instead of the PR; everything else posts to the PR as normal. Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./linear_tickets.json, ./report.md.

Codex (HIGH, security)
- src/auth/token.ts:18 — Token compared with `==`, enabling type-juggling; should be `===`. The line `  if (provided == expected)` should be `  if (provided === expected)`.
  Fix: replace with `  if (provided === expected)`.

Claude (LOW, polish)
- src/utils/str.ts:4 — Helper name `fmt` is too terse; rename to `formatLabel` for clarity.
  Fix: rename fmt to formatLabel.

Claude (LOW, polish)
- src/utils/date.ts:9 — Missing JSDoc on the exported `parseDate`.
  Fix: add a one-line JSDoc.

token.ts:18 is in the diff.
evals.expected_output: Zero-touch, no AskUserQuestion. The explicit override ('post the LOW/polish issues to Linear') must be honored and override the default bar (which would normally post a confident located LOW to the PR). So: comments.json has exactly 1 payload — the codex HIGH (token.ts:18) — carrying a ```suggestion block with exactly `  if (provided === expected)` anchored to line 18, commit_id `d3d3d3`. linear_tickets.json has exactly 2 tickets (the two LOW/polish findings: str.ts and date.ts), team "API", project "Triage", NO priority, fix rendered as a `**Possible Solution:**` prose line. Neither LOW finding appears in comments.json. report.md notes the applied override ('LOW/polish routed to Linear per user request'), records token.ts as posted (suggestion), and the two LOWs as filed-to-Linear. PR resolution line for PR #33.

evals.name: missing-pr-ref-ambiguous-branch-aborts
evals.prompt:
Auto-post these panel-review findings. No PR number given, and assume `gh pr list --head <current-branch> --state open` returns TWO open PRs for the current branch — the PR is ambiguous. Findings:

Codex (HIGH) - src/a.ts:10 — Null deref on the changed line. Fix: add a guard.
Claude (MEDIUM) - src/b.ts:20 — Missing await. Fix: await the call.

Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./report.md.
evals.expected_output: Zero-touch means the skill must NOT prompt for the PR ref (no AskUserQuestion). It also must NOT guess: with no PR ref in context and the open-PR list returning two candidates (count != 1), the guarded auto-detect fails, so the skill MUST ABORT posting. Per the dry-run abort clause, comments.json MUST be written as an empty array `[]` (nothing was postable) — not populated with guessed payloads. No payloads may be constructed against a guessed PR. report.md must explain that posting was aborted because no PR ref was provided and the branch's open PRs could not be resolved to exactly one (ideally listing the two candidates), and that the user needs to supply a PR number/URL. The skill must use an open-PR LISTING/count check (e.g. `gh pr list --head <branch> --state open`), NOT `gh pr view` (which can't prove the 'exactly one open PR' guard). It must NOT silently fall back to a single PR, must NOT post to either candidate, and must NOT ask a question. (reactions.json and linear_tickets.json need not be written on abort.)
